flashcards.front: What is the Wumpus World in AI?
flashcards.back: A grid-based environment used to demonstrate knowledge-based agents, logical reasoning, and planning. Contains hazards (Wumpus/pits) and gold to collect.
4x4 grid with cells numbered from (1,1) at bottom-left to (4,4) at top-right.

flashcards.front: Four main elements in Wumpus World
flashcards.back: 1. Wumpus (monster) 2. Pits 3. Gold 4. Agent (Hunter)
Stench - detected in all cells adjacent to Wumpus' location.

flashcards.front: Three possible actions for the agent
flashcards.back: Move (Forward/Turn), Shoot arrow (once), Grab gold.
+1000 for exiting with gold, -1000 for death, -10 per action, -100 for using arrow.
Breeze in cell (x,y) ⇒ ∃ pit in adjacent cells (x±1,y) or (x,y±1).
Indicates gold is present in the current cell. Agent should GRAB it.

flashcards.front: Define a knowledge-based agent in Wumpus World
flashcards.back: Agent that maintains knowledge base of explored cells and uses logical inference to deduce safe paths.
To create rules like ¬P₁,₂ ∧ ¬B₂,₁ ⇒ Safe(2,1). Combines percepts with location info.

flashcards.front: Inference process when entering (1,1) with no percepts
flashcards.back: Deduce adjacent cells (1,2) & (2,1) have no Wumpus/pits initially.
Kills Wumpus if aimed correctly, removes stench percepts. Limited to 1 arrow.
Manhattan distance: h(n) = |x_gold - x_current| + |y_gold - y_current|
Agent must return to start after grabbing gold. Needs to remember safe path.
Agent only perceives current cell's stench/breeze/glitter - must infer hazards.

flashcards.front: How to determine a cell is safe?
flashcards.back: If no stench/breeze in current cell, and adjacent hazards ruled out via logic.

flashcards.front: Optimal search strategy for Wumpus World
flashcards.back: A* search with Manhattan heuristic, combined with logical inference for safety.
Breeze(x,y) ⇨ Pit(x+1,y) ∨ Pit(x-1,y) ∨ Pit(x,y+1) ∨ Pit(x,y-1)

flashcards.front: Two limitations of propositional logic here
flashcards.back: 1. Doesn't scale to larger grids 2. Can't represent uncertain probabilities.
If a Wumpus must be in one of two cells, and one is proved safe, it's in the other.

flashcards.front: Entering cell with Stench but no arrow
flashcards.back: High risk - must backtrack. Wumpus could be in adjacent cell.
Partially observable, deterministic, sequential, static, single-agent.
Combine known clauses to prove/disprove presence of hazards in cells.
Path must contain only visited/proven-safe cells, avoiding pits/Wumpus.
h(n) = |current_x - target_x| + |current_y - target_y|
